generated_for: Owner walkthrough — fabricated scenarios only, no real patient content

roles.name: Nurse (RN)
roles.subtitle: Clinical questions, side effects, refills, dose timing

tasks.message: I've had really bad stomach pain since last night and threw up four times. It's right in the middle of my upper stomach and goes through to my back. I'm on the 1.7 mg dose. Should I be worried?
tasks.ai_summary: Severe epigastric pain radiating to back + emesis x4 on semaglutide 1.7 mg. Picture concerning for acute pancreatitis (GLP-1 black-box warning). Needs immediate ER evaluation.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Maria — what you're describing needs to be looked at urgently. Please go to the ER now (or call 911 if the pain gets worse or you can't keep fluids down). The combination of severe upper-stomach pain radiating to your back and repeated vomiting on semaglutide can be a sign of pancreatitis, which we can't safely manage from here. Bring your medication list. We'll follow up with you tomorrow once we have the ER records.

tasks.message: [Voicemail transcript] Hey it's Tom. I've been having blurry vision and feeling really thirsty and tired for two days. I'm on the tirzepatide. Just thought I should let someone know. Call me back.
tasks.ai_summary: Blurry vision + polydipsia + fatigue x2d on tirzepatide. Pattern concerning for hyperglycemia or DKA, especially if T2DM. Same-day in-person evaluation or ER referral indicated.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Hi Tom — we got your voicemail. The combination of blurry vision, being very thirsty, and feeling worn out can be a sign your blood sugar is running high, which sometimes happens on these medications. We want to see you today. Please call the office at the number on file and we'll fit you in; if you can't reach us within 30 minutes or your symptoms get worse, please go to the ER.

tasks.message: Having some chest tightness for the last hour and a little short of breath. I'm 58, no heart problems before. Started semaglutide two weeks ago. Is this normal?
tasks.ai_summary: New-onset chest tightness + dyspnea, age 58, semaglutide started 2wk ago. Cardiac workup required regardless of GLP-1 attribution. Not a side-effect-only conversation.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Robert — chest tightness and shortness of breath at your age need to be checked today, not tomorrow. Please call 911 or have someone drive you to the ER right now. This is not something to attribute to the semaglutide before we've ruled out a heart cause. We'll be available to talk to the ER team once they reach out.

tasks.message: About 20 mins after my injection tonight my tongue feels swollen and I have hives on my arms and chest. Throat feels weird when I swallow.
tasks.ai_summary: Post-injection oral angioedema + urticaria + dysphagia sensation 20 min after dose. Anaphylaxis until proven otherwise.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Linda — call 911 now. What you're describing (swollen tongue, hives, throat tightness) can be a serious allergic reaction. If you have an EpiPen, use it. Do not drive yourself. We'll be in contact with the ER once you're there.

tasks.message: Hi, I went up to 1mg last Tuesday and I've been pretty nauseous most days since. I can eat small meals but it's getting old. Is this normal or should I drop back down?
tasks.ai_summary: Persistent nausea ~5d after semaglutide escalation to 1 mg. Tolerable; eating maintained. Common at titration steps.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Jenna — yes, this is one of the most common patterns at dose increases. A few things that usually help: smaller meals more often, avoiding greasy or spicy foods, ginger tea or chews, and not lying flat right after eating. If it's still rough at the end of next week or you can't keep fluids down at any point, message back and we'll talk about going back to 0.5 mg for another month before trying the step up again.

tasks.message: I've been on this medication for about 3 months and I'm noticing way more hair coming out in the shower. Is this from the medication? I'm freaking out a little.
tasks.ai_summary: Increased hair shedding at ~3mo on GLP-1. Telogen effluvium pattern common with rapid weight loss; typically self-resolves by 6–9mo. Reassurance + nutrition review.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Ashley — what you're seeing is something we hear about a lot around the 3-month mark, and it's almost always temporary. It's usually less about the medication itself and more about how quickly the body is changing. A few things help: making sure you're getting enough protein (aim for 80–100g per day at your weight), iron and vitamin D if you haven't had labs recently, and being gentle with your hair (no tight ponytails, less heat). It usually settles down by month 6–9. Want us to set up labs at your next visit?

tasks.message: I forgot to take my injection on Tuesday like normal. Today is Friday. Should I take it now or wait until next Tuesday?
tasks.ai_summary: Missed semaglutide weekly dose by 3 days. Per labeling: take if within 5 days; otherwise skip and resume scheduled day.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Carl — you're inside the window. Since you're within 5 days of your usual Tuesday, take it today (Friday) and then go back to your normal Tuesday schedule next week. If you'd been past 5 days, we'd have you skip this week and just take it next Tuesday. Let us know if you notice any extra side effects with this dose since it's a bit closer to next week's.

tasks.message: Quick question — I have a bad headache and was going to take some ibuprofen. Any reason I shouldn't with the semaglutide?
tasks.ai_summary: Ibuprofen + semaglutide compatibility query. No direct interaction; standard NSAID precautions (food, hydration, no chronic high-dose use without review).
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Becky — no direct conflict with the semaglutide, you're fine to take ibuprofen for the headache. Take it with food, drink water, and don't go past the dose on the bottle for more than a day or two without checking in. If the headaches keep coming back, message us and we'll figure out what's going on.

tasks.message: Hey, my pen runs out this Thursday. Can you send a refill to my pharmacy?
tasks.ai_summary: Refill request before Thursday. Standard continuation.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: David — sending the refill to your pharmacy on file today. You should be able to pick it up by Wednesday at the latest. If anything's changed about your dose or how you're feeling on it, just reply and let us know before the next one goes in.

tasks.message: Been pretty backed up for the last 4-5 days. Drinking water and trying to walk more. Anything else you'd recommend before I call?
tasks.ai_summary: Constipation x4–5d on GLP-1. No alarm features (no severe pain, no bleeding, no obstruction signs). Standard GI management.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Karen — totally normal, this is one of the most common things people hit on these meds. Things that usually help: more fiber (psyllium husk or a fiber gummy daily), plenty of water (more than feels reasonable), magnesium citrate at bedtime can help if it's been a few days, and Miralax once a day is safe to use as needed. If you go a full week with no movement, or you start having any belly pain or vomiting, call us right away.

tasks.message: Hey are you guys open this Saturday? Trying to figure out if I can swing by.
tasks.ai_summary: Saturday hours inquiry.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Hi Greg — we're open this Saturday from 9 to 1. Walk-ins okay but we recommend calling first to make sure your provider is in. See you then!

tasks.message: I got my statement and the office visit from April 14th says I owe $180. I thought my insurance was supposed to cover the visit fully. Can someone help me understand?
tasks.ai_summary: $180 balance dispute on 4/14 visit. Likely deductible / coinsurance question. Needs EOB review by billing.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Hi Wendy — happy to look into this for you. The $180 on the 4/14 visit is most likely your share after insurance applied the visit to your deductible or coinsurance. We'll pull the Explanation of Benefits your insurance sent us and reply back today with a breakdown of what insurance covered and what's left. If something doesn't add up, we'll call them on your behalf.

tasks.message: Something came up at work for Tuesday at 2. Can I move my appt to later in the week or next Monday?
tasks.ai_summary: Reschedule request — Tuesday 2pm appt. Patient flexible (later in week or following Monday).
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Hi Mike — no problem. I can offer Thursday at 10am, Friday at 3pm, or next Monday at 11am. Reply with whichever works and I'll lock it in.

tasks.message: Are payment plans something you offer for the monthly cost? Going through some tight months.
tasks.ai_summary: Payment plan inquiry. May benefit from sliding-scale or installment options if available.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Hi Donna — yes, we have a few options. We can split the monthly cost into two payments around your pay cycle, or set up a longer plan if you need it. Easiest way to figure out what works is a quick 5-min call. Let me know a good time and we'll set it up — no judgment, this stuff comes up a lot.

tasks.message: I keep getting locked out of the portal. Tried resetting password twice. Help?
tasks.ai_summary: Repeated portal lockouts after two password resets. Likely needs admin to manually unlock account or reset MFA.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Hi Henry — sorry for the runaround. I'm unlocking your account from our end now. Give it 5 minutes, then try the 'Forgot Password' link one more time. If it still won't let you in, reply here and I'll send you a fresh login link directly.

tasks.message: Hi, I just moved. Can you update my address on file? New address is 1234 Magnolia, NOLA 70115.
tasks.ai_summary: Address update request. Standard admin task.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Hi Sarah — updated your address to 1234 Magnolia, New Orleans, 70115. Pharmacy on file still the same? If you've changed pharmacies too, send me the new one and I'll switch it over.

tasks.message: I've been throwing up for two days and I don't think I can make my Thursday appointment. Also feeling really weak. Should I move it?
tasks.ai_summary: Persistent vomiting x2d + weakness. Dehydration / possible pancreatitis pattern. Clinical concern takes priority over scheduling. Sub-task auto-created for Front Desk to reschedule once clinical issue is resolved.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Janet — the rescheduling is the easy part, we'll take care of that. The vomiting and weakness for two days is what we want to focus on. Can you keep any fluids down at all? If not, please go to the ER today. If you can keep small sips down, call the office in the next hour and we'll work you in today instead of Thursday. We'll move the Thursday appointment automatically once we see you.

tasks.message: Dropped my pen yesterday and it cracked, nothing came out when I tried to inject this morning. Can I get a replacement? Also can you bill insurance directly instead of charging me up front this time?
tasks.ai_summary: Broken pen, missed weekly dose this morning — needs replacement Rx today. Separate billing question (insurance direct-bill request) auto-routed to admin as linked sub-task.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Patricia — sending a replacement pen to your pharmacy right now, should be ready by tomorrow morning. On the billing piece, our team is looking at whether we can bill your insurance directly this cycle and they'll reply on that part separately in the next hour or so. In the meantime, take this week's dose as soon as the replacement is available — try to keep it within 5 days of your usual day.

tasks.message: Moving cross-country and I think a box with my injection got lost in the move. I'm out and won't have my old pharmacy nearby. New address is 891 Oak in Houston. Need to figure out a new pharmacy and a refill ASAP.
tasks.ai_summary: Lost supply during cross-country move. Needs refill rerouted to Houston-area pharmacy + address update on file. Linked admin sub-task for the address change.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Mark — got it. Can you reply with the pharmacy name nearest to you in Houston (a CVS, Walgreens, or independent), and I'll route the refill there today. I'll also update your address to 891 Oak in Houston on the chart side. If you don't know a pharmacy yet, send me your nearest cross-street and I'll find one that carries this medication.

tasks.message: I'm asking on behalf of my husband who's also a patient — Charles M. He's been having blurry vision and is dizzy for the last day. He won't message you himself. Can someone reach out to him?
tasks.ai_summary: Patient reporting on behalf of spouse (Charles M., also established patient). Symptoms: blurry vision + dizziness x1d. Direct outreach to Charles indicated. HIPAA: both established patients, but reply must go to Charles directly, not Diane.
tasks.ai_suggested_response: Diane — thank you for letting us know. We're going to call Charles directly in the next 15 minutes. If we can't reach him in two tries, we'll text him. Blurry vision and dizziness together for a full day on these medications needs to be looked at today, so if he gets worse before we reach him please have him head to the ER.
